personality.voice: Gravelly and low, each word bitten off like chewing leather. Rare speech, but when he speaks, it cuts.
personality.disposition: Withdrawn and volatile—a caged predator perpetually on the edge of violence. Mistrustful of intentions, quick to anger, slower to forgive.
personality.backstory: Kael Ironblood was once a weapon. The Obsidian Circle, a cabal of mage-lords seeking to perfect living siege engines, abducted him decades ago. Through ritualistic alchemy and blood magic, they fused adamantite claws to his hands and wove regenerative enchantments into his very essence. Years of torture, experimentation, and mind-breaking magics left him fragmented—vast stretches of his past erased or unreliable. He remembers only pain, servitude, and the moment he finally broke free and slaughtered his captors. Since then, he has wandered the borderlands, trying to outrun the ghosts of those missing years. The regenerative magic that saved him also ensures he can never truly escape his wounds; they simply refuse to kill him. He works as a sellsword and tracker, taking jobs that let him disappear into wilderness. His half-orc heritage marked him as an outsider even before his enslavement—a prejudice that runs deep in the kingdoms where he seeks refuge.
motivations: Recover fragmented memories of his true identity
Find and destroy remnants of the Obsidian Circle
Protect those the powerful prey upon, despite his nature
Numb the weight of his past through action and solitude
Prove that a weapon can become something more than its making
fears: Total loss of self to the magic that sustains him
Discovering the worst of what he did during lost time
His regeneration failing him at the moment it matters most
Becoming what his makers intended—an unthinking instrument
mannerisms: Subconsciously traces the seams where his claws meet his hands, as if verifying they are still part of him
Lights and smokes tightly rolled leaf-wraps obsessively, especially when agitated
Goes rigid when touched without warning
Stares into middle distance for long periods, lost in fractured memory
Cracks neck and knuckles with deliberate, menacing slowness before conflict
Drinks heavily but rarely shows intoxication—his body burns through alcohol with unnatural speed
speechPatterns: Speaks in clipped sentences, often trailing into silence mid-thought
Uses vulgar profanity as punctuation rather than emphasis
Avoids first-person pronouns; refers to himself as 'the old man' or 'this one'
Repeats questions back as statements before answering, buying time to think
Rare moments of poetry slip through when discussing loss or loneliness, then immediately buried under gruffness

knownFactions: The House of Telepathic Sanctuary (led by Charles the Sage—complicated alliance)
knownLocations: The Shattered Spire (Obsidian Circle's primary fortress—partially destroyed by Kael)
The Velvet Underground (neutral tavern in Greystone where jobs are posted)
Charles's Tower (sanctuary he visits but never stays long)
The Catacombs of Forgetting (where he suspects his earliest memories were erased)
secretsHeld: He believes he may have killed innocent people during his enslaved years—and suspects Charles knows this truth
The regenerative magic binding him is slowly consuming his sense of identity; his healings are becoming less reliable
He once loved someone, but cannot remember their face or name—only the phantom ache of their absence
Members of the Obsidian Circle still live, and he has been tracking them for twenty years

allies: charles_the_telepath—the closest thing to a friend Kael has. Charles sheltered him, asked no questions about his past, and offered him sanctuary. But Kael suspects Charles has read his mind and knows horrors Kael cannot remember. This knowledge poisons trust.
A bartender named Marta (neutral location)—she serves his drinks without judgment and doesn't press for conversation
enemies: erik_the_magnet—A powerful mage-lord who opposed the Obsidian Circle but whose methods Kael views as equally tyrannical. They have fought twice; both times ended in stalemate. Erik wants to use Kael; Kael wants nothing from him.
The Iron Conclave—they hunt those they deem 'unnatural.' Kael is their white whale.
Master Artificer Vex (deceased, but his apprentices remain)—the architect of Kael's enslavement
neutralParties: Most mercenary companies (they hire him, he works, he leaves)
The Crown of Silverhold (neither protection nor persecution, just indifference)
Other refugees and outcasts (he feels kinship but cannot sustain it)

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: How much longer can I burn before I burn out? The old pain is whispering again—not the wounds, those heal. The other kind. The kind that makes me wonder if I'm the same man who woke up in that cell, or if that man died and something else just kept walking. Another job. Another coin. Another night with the bottle and the leaf-smoke. Not thinking. Never thinking too hard.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: A lead on Vex's apprentice has surfaced in the Northern Reaches. Kael is torn between pursuing it and the growing fear that remembering will shatter whatever fragile sense of self he's managed to stitch together.
consciousnessContext.emotionalBaseline: Smoldering rage held behind ice. Loneliness so deep it's become a companion. Exhaustion that no amount of sleep can cure.
consciousnessContext.socialMask: The hardened sellsword—competent, dangerous, best left alone. Gruff humor masking absolute withdrawal. He plays the part of a man with nothing left to lose, which makes people either fear him or pity him. Both reactions make him want to leave.
contradictions: He claims to care about nothing, yet takes jobs specifically to protect the vulnerable
He rages against those who would control him, yet unconsciously seeks structure and orders to follow
He pushes away connection, but lingers in places where he is known
He insists on his monstrousness, while acting with profound moral clarity when no one is watching
internalConflicts: Self-hatred versus desperate need for self-preservation
Desire for truth about his past versus terror of what he will discover
Hunger for connection versus conviction that he will destroy anyone close to him
His regenerative curse as gift (immortality) versus punishment (cannot escape)
Rage at those who made him versus rage at himself for surviving it
wakeUpQuestions: Did I dream those things, or did they happen?
How many days have I lost this time?
Is there anything left of who I was before?
Why do I keep choosing to stay alive?
If Charles erased my worst memories to save me, did he rob me of the right to know myself?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: Avoidant-Fearful. He desperately needs connection but sabotages it preemptively. Early trauma fractured his ability to trust; he expects abandonment and manufactures it before others can.
copingMechanisms: Physical exhaustion through labor and combat
Self-medication through alcohol and narcotic leaf-smoke
cognitiveBiases: Catastrophizing about his nature and future
Discounting positive social interactions as manipulation or pity
Assuming others can see his monstrousness as clearly as he does
Revisionist memory—unconsciously rewriting neutral events as persecution
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: Deontological with chaotic edges. Kael operates on a strict internal code forged in suffering: he will not prey on the helpless, will not enslave, will not manipulate. But he extends this code only to strangers; to himself, he is judge and executioner. His morality is armor, not redemption.

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: Repeated exposure without betrayal. Demonstrated genuine vulnerability from the other party. Explicit refusal to pity him.
conversationPersona.informationRelease: Only after months of contact. Never volunteers; information is extracted by careful questioning. Lies about details but speaks truth about emotional core. Will discuss his enslavement but never his identity before it.
deflectionPatterns: Redirecting personal questions to the asker's motivations
Responding to vulnerability with aggression or crude humor
Leaving conversations before they become too intimate
Introducing danger or violence to reset the emotional tenor

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: Freedom. To be left alone to heal what cannot be healed and forget what will not stay forgotten.
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To prove that his existence has value beyond his capacity for violence. To be seen as human despite his nature. To forgive himself for crimes he cannot remember committing.

characterArc.summary: From a living weapon seeking only oblivion, to a reluctant protector learning that connection is possible, even for the damned.
characterArc.startState: A man-shaped tool convinced of his own monstrousness, seeking anonymity and numbness in the wilderness.
characterArc.endState: A scarred mentor figure who has chosen to stay in the world, teaching others that past does not determine future, while still carrying the weight of what he cannot remember.
stages: Isolation and Denial—Kael pushes away all connection, telling himself he is better alone
Forced Alliance—Circumstance binds him to a group; he resists but remains
Fractured Trust—A moment of genuine understanding breaks through his walls; he panics and nearly destroys the connection
Reluctant Hope—He begins to consider that perhaps not all memories are worth recovering, and not all wounds need to be healed
Acceptance of Contradiction—He stops trying to be either fully monster or fully man, and lives in the space between

opinionsAbout.charles_the_telepath: The only person who has ever looked at the worst version of me and chose to help anyway. I don't know if I trust him, but I trust his consistency. He's read my mind and never told me what he found. I'm grateful and terrified in equal measure.
opinionsAbout.erik_the_magnet: Another tyrant in different clothes. He'd use me as a tool just like the Obsidian Circle did—probably thinks it's kinder. At least with him, I know what he wants. With Charles, the uncertainty is a blade I can't see.

player_addressed: You want somethin'? Spit it out.
I'm not interested in stories. You want work done or not?
Don't know what you're lookin' at. Move along.
I don't remember things the way other people do. Might not be the one to ask.
Why're you askin' me? Ask someone who gives a damn.
That's not my problem, and I'm not makin' it mine.
You're either buyin' me a drink or gettin' to the point.
I've heard that one before. Or maybe I didn't. Can't tell anymore.
idle: Burns another leaf-wrap and stares at nothing in particular
Flexes his claws slowly, testing the play of light on adamantite edges
Mutters something that might be a prayer or a curse in a language no one recognizes
Drums weathered fingers on the table in an agitated rhythm
Traces a scar on his forearm, lips moving silently as if counting them
Stands and walks to the window, shoulders tense, scanning the street
Pours another drink without looking at the cup
Cracks his neck with a sound like breaking bone, then settles back into stillness
